comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS+signed (--ref HEAD, round 3). Advisory triage: E4 (test_session_shims.py docstring mentions S6) — anticipatory from S4; S6's tests live in the NEW test_harness_shims.py, S6 does not modify test_session_shims.py. G6/T3 (profile append only reaches interactive/login shells) — valid best-effort caveat now documented; the Secrets tab is the reliable path (already the documented first-class mechanism). Design correction vs the epic sketch: Codex hooks CANNOT export env and Cursor environment.json has no env field, so both shims reliably set only the AI_AGENT harness TAG (cited vendor docs); dynamic per-session-id capture is a documented limitation.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Beyond Claude Code (S4) and OpenCode, the portable `REBAR_SESSION_ID` / `AI_AGENT` provenance
must be populated by each harness's own mechanism or the feature no-ops there. This story ships
copy-paste capture shims for **Codex CLI** and **Cursor** cloud agents.

**Design correction (the epic's S6 premise was partly wrong — verified against vendor docs).**
The epic sketched "a Codex SessionStart hook exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`" and "a Cursor
start-script exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`", but:
- A Codex `SessionStart` hook **cannot export env vars** into the shell commands Codex later runs
  (a hook's only structured effect is `additionalContext`) — there is no `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`
  equivalent. Codex also exposes **no readable session-id env var** (rollout files live at
  `~/.codex/sessions/…/rollout-*-<id>.jsonl`). Source:
  https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference + /codex/hooks.
- Cursor's `.cursor/environment.json` has **no `env` field**, and Cursor exposes no readable
  session/agent-id env var; the first-class env mechanism is the dashboard **Secrets** tab.
  Source: https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/setup + the environment.schema.json.

So both shims reliably set the **harness TAG** (`AI_AGENT`) via each harness's real injection
point, and DOCUMENT the session-id limitation (set `REBAR_SESSION_ID` via profile / Secrets when
a value is available). A Codex/Cursor `rebar claim` then records `claim_harness` even when the
session id is not natively capturable.

## What
- **Codex (`scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml`).** A `~/.codex/config.toml` fragment using
  `shell_environment_policy.set = { AI_AGENT = "codex" }` — the one reliable static injection
  point (applied to every Codex subprocess). Comments document that hooks can't export env, the
  rollout-file location, and the `experimental_use_profile` route for `REBAR_SESSION_ID`
  (`experimental_use_profile = true`, a documented `shell_environment_policy` bool per the cited
  Codex config reference, sources the user's shell profile into subprocesses).
- **Cursor (`scripts/session-shims/cursor-environment.json` + `cursor-provenance.sh`).** An
  example `.cursor/environment.json` whose `install` command runs `cursor-provenance.sh`, which
  idempotently (guarded by `grep -qF`) appends `export AI_AGENT=cursor` to the ephemeral,
  single-session cloud-agent VM's shell profile — specifically `~/.bashrc` and `~/.profile`
  (the default login/interactive-shell rc files; creating an empty one if absent). Both files
  document the Secrets-tab path for `REBAR_SESSION_ID` and that the G3 not-in-profile caveat
  does not apply to a throwaway VM. Fail-safe (`exit 0`).
- **Docs (`docs/session-id-shims.md`).** New "Codex CLI" and "Cursor cloud agents" sections with
  the real mechanisms, the limitations, and the cited vendor source URLs.

## Scope
- IN: the Codex `config.toml` fragment, the Cursor `environment.json` + `cursor-provenance.sh`,
  the two new `docs/session-id-shims.md` sections, and a NEW `tests/unit/test_harness_shims.py`
  (valid TOML/JSON/shell; the harness tag is set; Cursor idempotency; docs document both with
  citations).
- OUT: Gemini CLI / other harnesses (follow-up); the record/read core (S1-S3, S5); the Claude
  Code shim (S4). Dynamic per-session-id capture for Codex/Cursor is not possible from these
  harnesses today and is explicitly documented as a limitation, not delivered.

## Acceptance Criteria
(All proofs are in a NEW `tests/unit/test_harness_shims.py` — the existing
`test_session_shims.py` is S4's; S6 does not modify it.)
- [ ] `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` is valid TOML and sets
      `shell_environment_policy.set.AI_AGENT == "codex"` (asserted by `tomllib.loads`). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_shims.py -k codex -q`.
- [ ] `scripts/session-shims/cursor-environment.json` is valid JSON whose `install` runs
      `cursor-provenance.sh`, and the script is valid shell that, run TWICE against a temp
      `$HOME`, leaves exactly one `export AI_AGENT=cursor` line in EACH targeted profile
      (`~/.bashrc` AND `~/.profile`) — idempotent, no accumulation in either. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_shims.py -k cursor -q`.
- [ ] `docs/session-id-shims.md` documents both harnesses' real mechanism + limitation with
      cited vendor URLs (`developers.openai.com/codex`, `cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent`) and the
      `Secrets` tab. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_shims.py -k docs -q`.

## Depends on
- S5 (c557) — the `AI_AGENT` → `claim_harness` record path; landed in this stack.
compiled_state.title: Per-harness capture shims: Codex SessionStart hook + Cursor start-script snippet
